prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response:
One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I think is overrated is the concept of "work-life balance." While it's essential to recognize the importance of having a healthy work-life balance, the emphasis on achieving equal parts of each can be misleading. In knowledge work, which often involves high levels of creativity and problem-solving, it's vital to have ample time to focus on work tasks without feeling guilty or neglecting personal life. At the same time, people must prioritize self-care and engage in activities that contribute to their overall well-being. 
Furthermore, the idea that work-life balance can be achieved through strict scheduling or rigid boundaries disregards the fact that work in the knowledge economy is often unpredictable and requires flexibility. Instead of striving for perfect balance, it's more effective to be adaptable and prioritize tasks based on their importance and urgency.
